task_completion.description: Create the Jupyter notebook and load the UrbanSim H5 data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Jupyter Notebook named 'space_efficiency_analysis.ipynb' is open. Code cells should show the loading of the data file from '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5' (e.g., using pandas HDFStore or read_hdf).

task_completion.description: Filter data, aggregate by zone, and calculate space efficiency metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells showing data manipulation: filtering for residential buildings (e.g., 'residential_units > 0'), filling missing residential square footage with zero, aggregating data (households, buildings, parcels) by zone, and calculating 'sqft_per_capita' as total_residential_sqft / total_persons.

task_completion.description: Export the zone-level summary to a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code cell showing the dataframe being exported to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_space_efficiency.csv'. The code should indicate the inclusion of columns: zone_id, total_buildings, total_residential_sqft, total_persons, and sqft_per_capita.

task_completion.description: Generate and save a scatter plot of total_persons vs. total_residential_sqft.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code cell generating a scatter plot with total_persons and total_residential_sqft, saving it to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/efficiency_scatter.png'. The executed cell must show the scatter plot visibly rendered in the notebook output.

task_completion.description: Generate and save a bar chart of the top 10 least efficient zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code cell filtering zones for at least 50 persons, selecting the top 10 with the highest sqft_per_capita, and plotting a bar chart. The code must save the plot to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/least_efficient_zones.png', and the bar chart must be visibly rendered in the notebook output.
